name: Foz das Aceas Trail
name_original: Ruta da Foz das Aceas – circular desde Sarria
description: Jog this moderate 5.7-mile circular route from Sarria, enjoying riverside paths, lush forests, and the historic Aceñas Bridge.
region_intro: Jogging around Sarria offers a chance to explore the region's varied landscape, characterized by rolling hills, river valleys, and lush woodlands. The area features a network of paths, often following the course of the Sarria River or traversing agricultural land. Elevation changes are generally gentle, providing accessible running experiences for various fitness levels. Best jogging routes around Sarria The most popular jogging route is Foz das Aceas Trail, a 5.7 miles (9.1 km) trail that takes 1 hour 4 minutes to complete. This route follows the river, offering views of the surrounding countryside. Another top favourite among local runners is Puente de las Aceñas loop from Sarria, a moderate 5.2 miles (8.4 km) path. This running route explores the riverbanks and local bridges, providing a scenic experience. Local runners also love the Ribeira Sacra Promenade loop from Sarria, a 4.2 miles (6.8 km) trail leading through riverine landscapes, often completed in about 44 minutes. Jogging around Sarria is defined by green river valleys, gentle hills, and woodland paths. The network offers options for different ability levels, from shorter, flatter runs to more challenging routes with moderate climbs. The routes in Sarria are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 5 reviews. More than 50 runners have used komoot to explore Sarria's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Sarria?
A: There are &lt;strong&gt;5 dedicated running routes&lt;/strong&gt; around Sarria listed in this guide, ranging from moderate to difficult. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars.

Q: Are there any circular jogging routes in Sarria?
A: Yes, many of the running paths around Sarria are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17680878" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de las Aceñas loop from Sarria&lt;/a&gt; offers a scenic 5.2-mile (8.4 km) run along riverbanks and bridges, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38146940" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribeira Sacra Promenade loop from Sarria&lt;/a&gt; is a 4.2-mile (6.8 km) option through riverine landscapes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on Sarria's jogging trails?
A: Sarria's jogging trails primarily feature &lt;strong&gt;rolling hills, river valleys, and lush woodlands&lt;/strong&gt;. The paths often follow the Sarria River or traverse agricultural land. Elevation changes are generally gentle, making them accessible for various fitness levels, though some routes include moderate climbs.

Q: Are there any running routes around Sarria that are suitable for beginners?
A: While most routes are classified as moderate, the gentle elevation changes and well-defined paths make many of them suitable for beginners looking for a bit of a challenge. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38146940" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribeira Sacra Promenade loop from Sarria&lt;/a&gt; is a shorter option at 4.2 miles (6.8 km) that can be a good starting point.

Q: Can I find jogging routes around Sarria with good viewpoints?
A: Yes, several routes offer scenic views of the Galician countryside. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991358972/foz-das-aceas-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foz das Aceas Trail&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, follows the river and provides picturesque views of the surrounding landscape. For more expansive vistas, consider the longer &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38146712" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miradoiro de San Pedro loop from Ferreiros&lt;/a&gt;, which includes a viewpoint.

Q: Are there any attractions or landmarks to see along the running routes near Sarria?
A: Absolutely! Many routes pass by interesting sights. You might encounter natural monuments like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/424987" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ancient Chestnut Tree in Ramil&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5911863" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ancient tree on the Camino Francés&lt;/a&gt;. Other notable landmarks include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4497257" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Shell-Shaped Fountain on the Camino de Santiago&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/769964" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;100 km Marker on the Camino de Santiago&lt;/a&gt;, reflecting the region's Camino heritage.

Q: What do other runners say about the jogging trails in Sarria?
A: The running trails in Sarria are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of &lt;strong&gt;5.0 stars from 5 reviews&lt;/strong&gt;. Runners often praise the quiet woodlands, the scenic river paths, and the overall accessibility of the routes for various fitness levels.

Q: Are there any running routes around Sarria that are suitable for families?
A: The generally gentle elevation changes and well-maintained paths make many of Sarria's routes suitable for families looking to jog together. Shorter, moderate loops like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38146940" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribeira Sacra Promenade loop from Sarria&lt;/a&gt; are good options for a family outing.

Q: Is Sarria a good place for longer distance running, like 10k or more?
A: Yes, Sarria offers routes suitable for longer distances. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991358972/foz-das-aceas-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foz das Aceas Trail&lt;/a&gt; is approximately 9.1 km (5.7 miles), making it a great option for a 10k run. For an even longer challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38146712" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miradoiro de San Pedro loop from Ferreiros&lt;/a&gt; extends to 19.1 km.

Q: Are there any running routes near Sarria that are accessible by public transport?
A: Sarria is a significant stop on the Camino de Santiago, which means it has good public transport connections, particularly by bus, to other towns in Galicia. Many of the routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17680878" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de las Aceñas loop from Sarria&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38146940" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribeira Sacra Promenade loop from Sarria&lt;/a&gt;, start directly from Sarria town, making them easily accessible if you arrive by public transport.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Sarria?
A: The best time to go jogging in Sarria is generally during the &lt;strong&gt;spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October)&lt;/strong&gt;. During these seasons, the weather is typically mild and pleasant, with less rain and comfortable temperatures for outdoor activities. Summer can be warm, while winter can bring cooler temperatures and more precipitation.
tips:
The Church of Santa Mariña in Sarria has a rich and fascinating history. It was built in the 12th century, during the rise of the Romanesque style in Galicia. The church is dedicated to Santa Mariña, a Christian martyr who is highly venerated in the region.

Over the centuries, the church has been an important reference point for pilgrims walking the Camino de Santiago, as Sarria is one of the most popular stages of this route. The church has undergone several restorations and modifications over time, but still retains original elements of its Romanesque architecture, such as its decorated arches and capitals.
Bridge that crosses the River Sarria, a nice walk.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Route of Foz das Aceas?
faqs.answer: The Komoot estimate for jogging the 9.1 km (5.7 miles) circular route is approximately 1 hour and 4 minutes. However, depending on your pace and stops to enjoy the scenery, completion times can range from 2 hours 7 minutes to 3 hours 3 minutes.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The route features verdant, hilly landscapes and riverside paths along the Sarria River. You'll encounter lush forests with chestnut, oak, alder, and maple trees, alongside green meadows and rolling hills. The paths are generally described as easily accessible.

faqs.question: Is the Foz das Aceas route suitable for beginners or those with limited fitness?
faqs.answer: While Komoot classifies it as 'moderate' and suggests 'good fitness' for jogging, the paths are generally easily accessible and suitable for most skill levels. Some sources even describe it as 'low difficulty', making it a simple and safe option for many.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and points of interest can I expect?
faqs.answer: You can expect picturesque views of the Sarria River, winding through the Foz das Aceas gorge. Along the way, you'll discover dams, old mills, and a waterfall. Highlights include the Santa Mariña Church, Sarria and the Aceñas Bridge. The rich vegetation and varied fauna add to its natural beauty.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: The route is conveniently accessible directly from the center of Sarria, suggesting parking options would be available within the town. Specific parking areas are not detailed, but starting from Sarria's center is the recommended approach.

faqs.question: Is the Route of Foz das Aceas dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally considered dog-friendly, offering a refreshing escape with natural sounds for both you and your pet. Always ensure your dog is under control, preferably on a leash, to respect wildlife and other trail users.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: The route is beautiful in any season. It offers cool shade in summer, making it pleasant for jogging, and vibrant colorful foliage in autumn. Spring brings fresh greenery, and even winter can offer a serene experience, though conditions may vary.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there is no mention of any permits or entrance fees required to access the Route of Foz das Aceas. It is a publicly accessible natural space.

faqs.question: Are there informative signs along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, informative signs are present along the route, providing details about the local flora and fauna, enhancing your understanding and appreciation of the natural surroundings.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths, including sections of the Camino Francés - 07 Cacabelos a Palas de Rei, O Camiño da Acea de Arriba, Paseo da Ribeira, Rúa Escalinata Maior, Paseo Río Sarria, Paseo do Malecón, A Ponte da Ribela, O Camiño das Ribeiras, and A Ponte do Chanto.

faqs.question: Is the Foz das Aceas route suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Given its generally accessible paths and classification as 'simple and safe' by some sources, it can be suitable for families. The varied scenery, including dams, mills, and a waterfall, can keep children engaged. Always consider your family's fitness level.
